note: Friday 5/15 daily scrape covering the 5/14-5/15 window. 4 new leads scoring 60+, all pr-marketing — this cycle's net-new business events were concentrated almost entirely in the 5/14 funding board. TOP NEW LEADS are EQUIPIFI ($34M Series B, bank-led BNPL infrastructure, Left Lane Capital) and NECTAR SOCIAL ($30M Series A, agentic AI marketing, ex-Meta founders, emerged from stealth) — both combined 77, B. ICEOTOPE ($26M Series B, precision liquid cooling for AI data centers, UK) and STITCH ($25M Series A, cloud-native OS for financial institutions, Saudi Arabia) round out pr-marketing as B leads with geographic caveats (both non-US-headquartered). FREELANCE PIPELINES: legal-freelance and pr-freelance produced no net-new company-specific postings in the daily window — job-board searches (Indeed/CareerBuilder/ZipRecruiter/Google-indexed LinkedIn) returned almost entirely aggregator platform pages (Upwork, Toptal, ZipRecruiter listing hubs), staffing-firm marketing pages, and general market commentary, not discrete company postings. Note that the dedicated legal-freelance scrape already ran today (2026-05-15, 3 leads: Ontra, Hayden Industrial, Interlink Cloud Advisors) and harvested that pipeline — the daily scrape adds nothing on top. SKIPPED: CREATE Medicines ($122M Series B 5/14) — genetic-medicines biotech, outside the FS/Tech pr-marketing ICP; Altara ($7M seed) — out of window (announced 5/5); Axiom Investment Funds Lawyer remote posting — staffing-firm placement of a ~$147k-start near-permanent role, and Axiom is already a tracked staffing entity; Ivy-to-Augustus fintech rebrand (post-OCC bank charter) — genuine PR trigger but rebrand timing not confirmed inside the 24-48h window, flagged for manual verification; Enjoin technology-first rebrand — healthcare revenue-integrity company, partial industry fit and rebrand already executed/rolling, thin sourcing. SIGNAL REFRESH: Greenboard's GreenboardGo launch is confirmed live with named customer proof points (Root Financial ~24 hrs/week reclaimed; JMG 60% onboarding-time cut) — press cycle still OPEN, outreach window favorable; Outmarket AI appointed Alpesh Patel (ex-Salesforce, ex-DocuSign) as CRO — a GTM-build / decision-maker signal, +3 intent (78->81, now A-tier); Exaforce confirmed growth to 130+ employees with Japan/Europe GTM expansion; Elliptic confirmed $120M Series D product-expansion plans; Exaforce and Elliptic both retain meaningful PR-incumbency risk. AUTONOMOUS-RUN NOTES: (1) all 4 new leads routed to pr-marketing as business-event (funding) leads, not job postings, per routing rules; (2) Stitch scored to a numeric B (60) but is effectively a C-priority given its MENA/GCC market focus vs. a US-based agency — flagged in its notes; (3) no leads suppressed for low score — the 4 included are all 60+; CREATE Medicines and Altara excluded for ICP/window reasons noted above; (4) contact names left as verify-placeholders where founder/exec names were not confirmable from coverage.

leads.name: equipifi
leads.industry: Fintech — native Buy Now, Pay Later (BNPL) and flexible-payment infrastructure for banks and credit unions
leads.fundingStage: Series B — $34M raised 2026-05-14, led by Left Lane Capital, continued participation from Curql and PHX Ventures

contacts.name: equipifi CEO (verify exact name from 5/14 Series B coverage — PYMNTS / FinSMEs)
contacts.title: CEO / Co-Founder

leads.source: Daily Scrape 2026-05-15 (PYMNTS / FinSMEs — equipifi Series B coverage)
leads.notes: Top new lead this cycle (combined 77, B). equipifi sells native BNPL/flexible-payment infrastructure to banks and credit unions — a clean FS/Tech B2B fit with an obvious enterprise sales motion. The $34M Series B (Left Lane Capital lead) is a strong, fresh trigger: the company has stated it will use the round to expand FI partnerships, deepen core product, and double headcount with a product/engineering focus — a classic growth-and-hiring inflection where PR demand (category leadership, partner announcements, executive positioning) tends to spike. CAVEAT: equipifi is a ~5-year-old Series B company in a competitive BNPL space and very likely already has PR support or an agency relationship — the realistic wedge is a specific workstream (e.g. partner-launch comms cadence, thought leadership for the bank-led-BNPL category, or exec positioning) rather than a full greenfield retainer. Verify CEO/CMO contact from the funding coverage before outreach. Outreach angle: congratulate on the Series B, lead with bank-led-BNPL category narrative and partner-announcement cadence.

leads.name: Nectar Social
leads.industry: AI / marketing technology — agentic operating system for modern marketing (social intelligence, community engagement, influencer tooling, customer experience, social analytics)
leads.fundingStage: Series A — $30M raised 2026-05-14, led by Menlo Ventures and Menlo's Anthology Fund; participation from True Ventures, GV, and Kinship Ventures

contacts.name: Nectar Social Co-Founders (two former Meta leaders — verify exact names from 5/14 stealth-emergence coverage)
contacts.title: Co-Founder / CEO

leads.source: Daily Scrape 2026-05-15 (TechCrunch / PitchBook — Nectar Social Series A coverage)
leads.notes: Combined 77, B. Nectar Social emerged from stealth on 2026-05-14 with a $30M Series A (Menlo Ventures lead), an ex-Meta founding team, and a product launch (the autonomous Nectar Agent) — a triple trigger (stealth emergence + funding + launch) that is about as strong as the pr-marketing intent rubric rewards. Proof assets are unusually concrete for a Series A: 10M+ autonomous conversations/week, $100M in attributed revenue, named brand customers including e.l.f. CAVEAT — and it is a real one: Nectar Social is itself a marketing-technology company founded by former Meta marketing leaders, so the probability that it already has sophisticated in-house comms or an existing agency is high. This is a B, not an A, specifically because of that incumbency risk. Realistic wedge: earned-media / category-narrative support around 'agentic marketing,' or executive thought leadership for the founders — not a full comms build. Verify founder names before outreach.

leads.name: Iceotope
leads.industry: Data-center infrastructure — precision liquid cooling systems for high-density AI/GPU compute and edge infrastructure
leads.fundingStage: Series B — $26M raised 2026-05-14, led by Two Seas Capital and Barclays Climate Ventures; participation from Edinv, ABC Impact, Northern Gritstone, and British Business Bank / British Patient Capital

contacts.name: Iceotope Leadership (verify CEO / Head of Marketing from 5/14 Series B coverage — Tech.eu / FinSMEs)
contacts.title: CEO

leads.source: Daily Scrape 2026-05-15 (Tech.eu / FinSMEs — Iceotope Series B coverage)
leads.notes: Combined 65, B. Iceotope raised a $26M Series B (Two Seas Capital + Barclays Climate Ventures) to scale precision liquid cooling for next-generation AI data-center infrastructure — a category with a strong current news tailwind (AI compute thermal bottleneck). The funding is a clean trigger and the company has quotable performance claims (up to 40% lower data-center power use, 96% less water). Scored lower than equipifi/Nectar on FIT because Iceotope is a hardware/infrastructure vendor rather than a classic FS/Tech B2B SaaS company — the industry match to Mark's ICP is partial — and it is UK-headquartered, which is a geographic caveat for a US-based agency. Still a legitimate B: the AI-infrastructure narrative is hot and a cooling company with credible numbers is well-positioned for earned media. Wedge: earned-media / analyst-relations support riding the AI-data-center-power story. Confirm Mark's appetite for UK-based clients.

leads.source: Daily Scrape 2026-05-15 (PYMNTS — Stitch Series A coverage)
leads.notes: Combined 60, B numerically but EFFECTIVELY A C-PRIORITY. Stitch is a Saudi Arabia-based fintech building a cloud-native core-banking OS for financial institutions — a real FS/Tech B2B fit with a clear enterprise sales motion, and the $25M Series A is a genuine trigger. It is included because it clears the 40+ threshold and the industry fit is real. However, Stitch is headquartered in Saudi Arabia and explicitly focused on GCC/MENA expansion; the likelihood that a US-based boutique agency is the right PR partner for a regionally-focused MENA fintech is low. Intent sub-scores were kept conservative for that reason. Recommend Katie treat this as a watch-list / awareness item rather than an active outreach target unless Mark has a specific GCC angle or referral path.
